mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.08% (near zero) signals an extended monetary easing regime. Sustained low rates compress equity discount rates and reduce borrowing costs for corporations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as long as FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with near-zero rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment (PAYEMS) signals robust labor market strength and consumer income growth, which sustains aggregate demand and corporate earnings. A month-over-month increase in PAYEMS of at least 50,000 persons predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by at least 50,000 persons month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 148,319 thousand as of April 2007
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of months meeting the trigger condition, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly measure consumer and construction demand. When both series decline simultaneously by more than 3% month-over-month, it signals demand contraction and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 3% MoM AND RSAFS declines > 3% MoM simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,555 thousand and RSAFS at 625,416 million as of March 2007
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with emerging demand weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both HOUST and RSAFS decline > 3% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 100.02 reflects stable supply-side capacity utilization. A sustained decline in INDPRO below 99.5 over two consecutive months signals supply-side weakness and reduced profit margins, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 99.5 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO < 99.5 for two consecutive months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL at 276.72 combined with elevated unemployment (UNRATE at 4.6%) creates stagflation risk. When CPI rises more than 0.5% month-over-month while UNRATE remains above 4.5%, the Federal Reserve faces policy constraints, producing negative equity returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 276.72 and UNRATE at 4.6% as of April 2007
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 52% of instances when both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) at 19,465 billion dollars reflects underlying economic momentum. A quarter-over-quarter decline in GDPC1 of more than 0.5% signals recession onset and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,465.195 billion as of Q1 2007
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of quarters with GDPC1 decline > 0.5% QoQ, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 4.6% reflects tight labor market conditions. A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration and demand weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over three months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1,555 thousand combined with retail sales (RSAFS) at 625,416 million measure consumer and construction demand momentum. When HOUST rises more than 5% month-over-month while RSAFS simultaneously rises more than 2% month-over-month, it signals robust demand acceleration, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 5% MoM AND RSAFS rises > 2% MoM simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,555 thousand and RSAFS at 625,416 million as of March 2007
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 45% of instances when both HOUST rises > 5% MoM and RSAFS rises > 2% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.
